questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. They attend / a meeting / in New York / in the upcoming weekend.
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} correct answer is “they attend”“They attend” is grammatically incorrect. Because given sentence talks about future event. Therefore, sentence should be in future continuous tense. Correct sentence is: they will be attending a meeting in New York in the upcoming weekend.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. throw spanner in the works
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} the correct answer is “spoil careful plans”.Spanner in the works means “to do something that prevents a plan or activity from succeeding”.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} the correct answer is “coqutte”.Correct spelling is “coquette”.Greave meaning: A piece of armour used to protect the shin.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the underlined group of words. o̲n̲e̲ ̲w̲h̲o̲ ̲m̲a̲k̲e̲s̲ ̲a̲n̲ ̲a̲c̲t̲i̲v̲e̲ ̲e̲f̲f̲o̲r̲t̲ ̲t̲o̲ ̲p̲r̲o̲m̲o̲t̲e̲ ̲h̲u̲m̲a̲n̲ ̲w̲e̲l̲f̲a̲r̲e̲ naturally loves his fellow man and is brimming with the first order desires to help.
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} correct answer is “philanthropist”.Pacifist: someone who opposes war or violence as a means of settling disputes.Amateur: A person who takes part in a sport or an activity for pleasure, not for money as a job.Philanthropist: A person who helps the poor, especially by giving them money.Hedonist: A person who believes that pleasure is the most important thing in life.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. Anita knows her.
options: img {max-width:100% !important} She is known to Anita.
img {max-width:100% !important} She is whom Anita know.
img {max-width:100% !important} Anita is known by her.
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important}

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. showing many bright colors that seem to change in different lights
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} correct answer is “iridescent”.Iridescent: showing many bright colors that seem to change in different lights.Invincible: too strong or powerful to be defeated.Irrational: not based on reason or clear thought.Clandestine: secret and often not legal.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. These carriers of bacteria and viruses cause many diseases like Yellow Fever, Malaria etc. so panchayats and municipalities are taking a lot of care to protect people from them. B. There are thousands of species of mosquitoes that are known to the people of the world. They mainly live in still water and other places where water is stored. C. Mosquitos are everywhere now. Villagers, as well as city dwellers are afraid of these small creatures. D. Usually, female and male mosquitoes take feed on plants or fruit honey, however female mosquitoes mostly suck the blood of humans.
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} the correct answer is “CBAD”.A sentence can never be start with “these”. Hence, option b and d is not possible.Hence, CBAD is correct arrangement.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Complete the sentence with an appropriate ANTONYM of the word mentioned in the bracket. The feature ___________(recedes) in the speech of those who reject local identity.
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} correct answer is “expand”.Recede: to move away and begin to disappear. (Syn: abate, lessen and fade)Expand: to become or to make something bigger.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. officials who have tried to ________ themselves in office.
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} correct answer is “entrenched”Entrench: to firmly establish something, especially an idea or a problem, so that it cannot be changed.Extend: to make something longer or larger.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Pocket an insult
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} correct answer is “Tolerate insult without protest”.Pocket an insult: to receive an affront without open resentment, or without seeking redress.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The nationalists followed a policy of non-violence to make their _____________successful.
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} correct answer is “struggle”.Sentence is talking about a policy of non-violence to make their struggle successful.So, struggle is the most appropriate answer.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the highlight segment in the given sentence. The milk has boiled over and falls into the stove.
options: img {max-width:100% !important} fall over in the stove
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} correct answer is “fallen onto the stoves”.Present perfect sentence is used before “and”. It means that milk has already spilled out of its container. So, falls into the stove is not correct. Here is the most appropriate answer should be “fallen onto the stoves” because action is already done.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the highlight segment in the given sentence. Mrs. Sharma couldn’t wait to trying out her new dress.
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} correct answer is “wait to try out”.In the given sentence the use of “trying” indicate that the sentence is in present continuous tense.Use of present continuous tense indicate that the action is going on. (Example: ram is going to temple).Here simple present should be use. Therefore, the correct should be “wait to try out”.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} correct answer is “Succinst”.Correct spelling is “succinct” which means “briefly and clearly expressed”.Dilettante: A person who is or seems to be interested in a subject, but whose understanding of it not very deep or serious.Pantomime: A performance using gestures and body movements without words.Tryst: A private romantic rendezvous between lovers.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate meaning of the word given in bracket in the given sentence. _____________(concerning) parliament attack there are many theories.
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} correct answer is “regarding”.Concerning: on the subject of or in connection with. (Syn: about, regarding)Pertaining: be appropriate, related, or applicable to.Convergent: coming closer togetherLogical: related to logic.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} correct answer is “gargentuan”.Correct spelling: gargantuan which means tremendous in size, volume, or degree. (Syn: gigantic, colossal, huge, massive.)Demolish: tear down, raze.Hilarious: extremely amusing.Magnificent: extremely beautiful, elaborate, or impressive.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} In the following questions a statement has been given with highlighted text. Select the options that can replace the text with correct idiom or phrase. The two cousins are always competing and arguing with each other.
options: img {max-width:100% !important} Stab someone in the back
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} the correct answer is “Break a lance with”.Break a lance with: to engage in a competition, battle, or confrontation, usually with someone.Be fed up with: annoyed, unhappy, or bored, especially with a situation that has existed for a long time.A black sheep: a disfavored or disrepute member of a group.Stab someone in the back: betray someone.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} The given sentence contains an error in tense form. Identify the error and choose the option that correctly rectifies the error. Councilor will going to call you later in the afternoon.
options: img {max-width:100% !important} Councilor goes to call you later in the afternoon.
img {max-width:100% !important} Councilor will go to call you later in the afternoon.
img {max-width:100% !important} Councilor was gone to call you later in the afternoon.
img {max-width:100% !important} Councilor is going to call you later in the afternoon.
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} correct answer is “Councilor is going to call you later in the afternoon”.“Will going” is not grammatically correct. In future tense “Will + verb(ing)” doesn’t come together.For events/action that will take place in near future, present continuous tense is used. (Example: Shila is going to Italy tomorrow.)Therefore, “councilor is going to call you later in the afternoon” is grammatically correct answer.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Asinine
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} correct answer is “rational”.Asinine: extremely stupid or foolish.Absurd: wildly unreasonable, illogical or inappropriate.Rational: based on or in accordance with reason or logic.Crass: showing no intelligence or sensitivity.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Ossified
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} correct answer is “Pliable”.Ossified: having become rigid or fixed in attitude or position.Rigid: unable to bend or be forced out of shape; not flexible.Pliable: easily bent; flexible.Stubborn: unwilling to change ideas or be influenced.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Oxygen fuels our living processes. It contracts our muscles, helps our cells to work, grow and regenerate, (1) ______ our brains, calms our nerves, helps the heart to pump, fights infection and (2) ______ immunity," says Dr Narendra Nath Jena, Director, Institute of Emergency Medicine, MMH R Centre, Madurai. Once air enters the nose or mouth, it gets humidified and reaches (3) ______ alveoli. The alveoli are tiny air sacs that help to (4) ______ oxygen and transfer it into the blood stream and also to pull out the carbon dioxide and push it out of the lungs. COVID-19 destroys (5) ______ alveoli by thickening the walls so gas exchange becomes difficult. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1.
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} the correct answer is “feeds”.The cloze test is in present tense. So, feeds will come in blank.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Oxygen fuels our living processes. It contracts our muscles, helps our cells to work, grow and regenerate, (1) ______ our brains, calms our nerves, helps the heart to pump, fights infection and (2) ______ immunity," says Dr Narendra Nath Jena, Director, Institute of Emergency Medicine, MMH R Centre, Madurai. Once air enters the nose or mouth, it gets humidified and reaches (3) ______ alveoli. The alveoli are tiny air sacs that help to (4) ______ oxygen and transfer it into the blood stream and also to pull out the carbon dioxide and push it out of the lungs. COVID-19 destroys (5) ______ alveoli by thickening the walls so gas exchange becomes difficult. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2.
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} the correct answer is “boosts”. boost: help or encourage (something) to increase or improve.Thrust: push suddenly or violently in a specified direction.Lower: less high in position.Treat: behave towards or deal with in a certain way.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Oxygen fuels our living processes. It contracts our muscles, helps our cells to work, grow and regenerate, (1) ______ our brains, calms our nerves, helps the heart to pump, fights infection and (2) ______ immunity," says Dr Mahendra Nath Jena, Director, Institute of Emergency Medicine, GGH R Centre, Chennai. Once air enters the nose or mouth, it gets humidified and reaches (3) ______ alveoli. The alveoli are tiny air sacs that help to (4) ______ oxygen and transfer it into the blood stream and also to pull out the carbon dioxide and push it out of the lungs. COVID-19 destroys (5) ______ alveoli by thickening the walls so gas exchange becomes difficult. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 3.
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} the correct answer is “the”.The sentence describes the process of air entering the nose or mouth, undergoing humidification, and then reaching a specific location. The word "alveoli" is used to fill in the blank.The most appropriate answer is “the”.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Oxygen fuels our living processes. It contracts our muscles, helps our cells to work, grow and regenerate, (1) ______ our brains, calms our nerves, helps the heart to pump, fights infection and (2)______ immunity," says Dr Narendra Nath Jena, Director, Institute of Emergency Medicine, MMH R Centre, Madurai. Once air enters the nose or mouth, it gets humidified and reaches (3) ______ alveoli. The alveoli are tiny air sacs that help to (4) ______ oxygen and transfer it into the blood stream and also to pull out the carbon dioxide and push it out of the lungs. COVID-19 destroys (5) ______ alveoli by thickening the walls so gas exchange becomes difficult. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 4.
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} the correct answer is “pull in”.Pull in: to reach a destination.Pull off: succeed in achieving or winning something difficult.Pull out: move out from the side of the road, or form its normal position in order to overtake.Pull through: get through an illness or other dangerous or difficult situation.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Oxygen fuels our living processes. It contracts our muscles, helps our cells to work, grow and regenerate, (1) ______ our brains, calms our nerves, helps the heart to pump, fights infection and (2) ______ immunity," says Dr Narendra Nath Jena, Director, Institute of Emergency Medicine, MMH R Centre, Madurai. Once air enters the nose or mouth, it gets humidified and reaches (3) ______ alveoli. The alveoli are tiny air sacs that help to (4) ______ oxygen and transfer it into the blood stream and also to pull out the carbon dioxide and push it out of the lungs. COVID-19 destroys (5) ______ alveoli by thickening the walls so gas exchange becomes difficult. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5.
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} the correct answer “these”.The most appropriate answer is “these”.
